description: Persistent issue tracker. Decider appends new issues from Curmudgeon reviews, marks resolved when wins.json no longer contains the flagged text.

issues.title: HOL-NARRATIVE c2 hole 1 (moderate): count drift between 70 (dome-claimed) and 72 (catalog) unreconciled across prose
issues.description: 129 hardcoded mentions of 70 vs 15 of 72 across rendered HTML. The Overview verdict-bars has a reconciling sentence (70+2-collision=72) but hundreds of prose mentions say "Of the 70 WINs reviewed", "all 70 WINs", etc. without distinguishing dome headline vs review denominator. Curmudgeon recommends: pick one approach — (a) name the denominator at each mention, or (b) recompute prose against {{TOTAL_WINS}}=72 everywhere and reframe the reconciling sentence. Either path eliminates the "your own counts disagree" attack.
issues.routing_reason: Wide-ranging prose fix (129 mentions across rendered HTML) — requires systematic search-and-replace with editorial decisions on denominator naming. Route to analyst for bundle with any 72/70 reconciliation work.

issues.title: HOL-NARRATIVE c2 hole 3 (moderate): stance statement opener amplifies prejudged-verdict attack surface
issues.description: The new "The world is a globe..." opener is the first thing readers see, amplifying the "decided before looking" attack that c1 originally flagged. Curmudgeon offers three options: (a) keep but label as "Editorial stance — for transparency", (b) reframe the 0-survive-scrutiny chip as a finding-to-test, (c) lead with methodology preface. Source is in build-scripts/generate-html.js (NEVER_PUSH). Requires human decision on editorial framing before any patch.
issues.location:
build-scripts/generate-html.js (generates ds-stance-statement

block) — NEVER_PUSH, operator must edit
issues.escalation_reason: Source is in build-scripts/generate-html.js (classified NEVER_PUSH per CLAUDE.md ownership table). Edit requires direct operator action in the git clone. Also an editorial/strategic decision that warrants operator approval before patch.

issues.title: HOL-NARRATIVE c2 hole 4 (minor): Timestamp Error tab (pos 8) narratively orphaned between Kill Shots and Predictions
issues.description: Tab order: Overview→Eval Guide→Model→Self-Contradictions→70 Wins→Live Power→Kill Shots→Timestamp Error→Predictions→Falsify→AI→Refs. Timestamp Error tab (methodology finding, adjacent to Code Analysis and AI Directives) sits between Kill Shots and Predictions, interrupting the test-cadence. Curmudgeon recommends moving Timestamp Error adjacent to Self-Contradictions tab or combining with Code Analysis into "Methodology Audit." Counter-case: tab is the OTS canary so visibility may justify its standalone position.
issues.routing_reason: Editorial/structural tab-ordering change. Analyst should evaluate whether canary visibility justifies current position vs moving adjacent to methodology-audit tabs.

issues.title: HOL-NARRATIVE c2 hole 5 (minor): no top-of-document reading guide for 12-tab architecture
issues.description: A reader arriving at the Overview cannot tell the tabs are in a deliberate sequence. Arrow nav + Five-questions preface partially fill the gap but no "Here is the argument arc" statement exists. Curmudgeon recommends a single paragraph near the top of the Overview naming the macro arc (methodology, self-contradictions, WIN-by-WIN, aggregate, kill shots, timestamp error, external tests, AI/conclusions). Can be added to sections.json:overview.html ps-detail block.
issues.routing_reason: Short prose addition to overview section. Analyst writes the reading-guide paragraph and proposes as EXP.
